reasoning:
Based on information leading up to the forecast date of March 1, 2026, Wagner Moura's probability of winning the Academy Award for Best Actor is very low. While he won Best Actor at the Cannes Film Festival and won the Golden Globe Award for Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Drama for his role in "The Secret Agent," his Oscars campaign suffers from significant empirical deficits. Most crucially, Moura entirely missed nominations for both the Screen Actors Guild (SAG) Awards and the BAFTA Awards. Historically, failing to secure a SAG nomination is disastrous for an actor's Oscar chances; no actor in modern history has won Best Actor without at least a SAG nomination. 

Furthermore, trade publications such as the Los Angeles Times and The Hollywood Reporter list Moura as sitting comfortably in 3rd or 4th place behind frontrunners like Timothée Chalamet (for "Marty Supreme") and Leonardo DiCaprio. Betting lines from late February 2026 also indicate Moura only captures roughly 5% of betting volume. Although Chalamet's shock loss at the BAFTA awards (to non-Oscar nominee Robert Aramayo) demonstrates some volatility in the race, Moura's lack of major precursor nominations leaves him unpositioned to capitalize on any frontrunner weakness. Given these deep structural disadvantages, his chance of winning remains an unlikely longshot, placing his probability functionally around 4%.
